t_ch: 哎，以前我没得选，只能继承家业。
t_en: *Sigh*... Before, I had no choice but to take on the family business.

t_ch: 现在…我虽只是个普通药商，但我想做个好人。
t_en: But now... I'm just a normal medicine trader, but I'd still like to be a good person.

t_ch: 生意的确重要，但凡药三分毒，没事啊还是别乱吃药，我也不能瞎开药害人。
t_en: Business is important, I know that. But every medicine has its side effects and shouldn't just be flogged off indiscriminately. I can't just prescribe potentially harmful drugs to anyone.

t_ch: 我好像在哪儿见过你。
t_en: Where have I seen you before?

t_ch: 怎么可能，一定是你看错了！
t_en: No way. You must be mistaken!

t_ch: 等等，你是在哪里看到我的？这不可能，我明明一直待在药店里……
t_en: Hang on, where do you think you saw me? That's impossible. I've definitely been here in my pharmacy the whole time...

t_ch: 我只是随口一提，你紧张什么。
t_en: I was just making conversation. Why so serious?

t_ch: 药不能乱吃，话也不能乱说，懂吗？做人不应该这样。
t_en: You can't just randomly take any medicine, just like you can't randomly talk nonsense, okay? Have a little integrity, please.

t_ch: 我见多识广，连景元都认识……
t_en: I've been around the block. Ever heard of my friend, Jing Yuan...?

t_ch: 噢，我一直佩服景元将军，还有云骑军的诸位。我见他们工作要时常通宵执勤，真不容易啊……
t_en: Oh, I'm a big fan of General Jing Yuan, and everyone in the Cloud Knights actually. I see they're always on-duty, working through the night. That's not easy...

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I'd better leave you to it.

t_ch: 太卜司的人曾给我算过一卦，说我会是大富大贵的命。可我不信，江湖行医嘛，为的不过就是一口饭而已。
t_en: Once, this diviner read my fortune and told me I'd be rollin' in dough and living large. But I ain't buying it. I mean, I'm just a wandering healer trying to make ends meet.

t_ch: 强求那些富贵做什么，出来贩药，迟早要还的…我只想做个普通的…哎……
t_en: Pursuing greed in medicine will eventually come back to bite you, sooner or later... I just want to live out an ordinary life... *Sigh*...

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I'd better leave you to it.

t_ch: 太卜司的人曾给我算过一卦，说我会是大富大贵的命。可我不信，江湖行医嘛，为的不过就是一口饭而已。
t_en: Once, this diviner read my fortune and told me I'd be rollin' in dough and living large. But I ain't buying it. I mean, I'm just a wandering healer trying to make ends meet.

t_ch: 强求那些富贵做什么，出来贩药，迟早要还的…我只想做个普通的…哎……
t_en: Pursuing greed in medicine will eventually come back to bite you, sooner or later... I just want to live out an ordinary life... *Sigh*...

t_ch: 欢迎光临，我们这里没有什么祖传秘方，只有常用的药和好用的药。
t_en: Welcome, welcome. We don't have any secret home recipes here. Just your everyday medicines and easy-to-use remedies.

t_ch: 如需…哦，是你呀，你有什么事吗？
t_en: If you need— Oh, it's you. What's up?

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 你好像在一直盯着外面看？
t_en: Why do you always seem to be peering outside?

t_ch: 我的店铺开在这儿，外面有什么风吹草动的，我看两眼很正常。
t_en: This is my shop, so it's normal for me to keep both eyes peeled for any troubles that might arise.

t_ch: 像我这种遵纪守法的小商人，要是遇见什么可疑的人和事了，我定然会及时上报到神策府去。
t_en: As a law-abiding small businessman, any suspicious character I come across is going to get reported to the Seat of Divine Foresight immediately.

t_ch: 听你口气，好像和神策府很熟？
t_en: Judging by your tone, you sound pretty familiar with the Seat of Divine Foresight?

t_ch: 咳——我之前和云骑军举报过几次化外民的可疑行为，仅此而已，你别瞎想。
t_en: *Ahem* I've reported a few suspicious Outworlders to the Cloud Knights before, that's all. Don't overthink it.

t_ch: 咱们罗浮的居民，还是得和云骑通力合作，这样才能保持现有的安宁嘛。这点自觉我还是有的。
t_en: Here on the Luofu, we still have to cooperate fully with the Cloud Knights to keep the peace around here. I'm self-aware enough to get that.

t_ch: 外面发生了什么事吗？
t_en: What happened outside?

t_ch: 那边的持明好像在嚷嚷什么，我也不清楚。
t_en: There're some Vidyadhara yelling about something over there. I don't really know what.

t_ch: 您要是听到些什么风声，倒是可以跟我说说，反正我闲着也是闲着嘛。
t_en: Fill me in if you hear any rumors out there. In any case, I'm just going to be stuck here.

t_ch: 能给我推荐点必备良药吗？
t_en: Can you recommend some good essential medicines for me?

t_ch: 我看您面色红润身手矫健，想来也没什么毛病，还是算了吧。
t_en: You look full of life and vigor. There's nothing wrong with you, so don't worry about it.

t_ch: 你这个态度不像卖药的……
t_en: You don't seem to care much about making sales at all...

t_ch: 连现成的生意都不做？
t_en: You don't even want to close this easy deal right in front of you?

t_ch: 哎，以前我没得选，只能继承家业。
t_en: *Sigh*... Before, I had no choice but to take on the family business.

t_ch: 现在…我虽只是个普通药商，但我想做个好人。

t_en: I'd better leave you to it.

t_en: Pursuing greed in medicine will eventually come back to bite you, sooner or later... I just want to live out an ordinary life... *Sigh*...

t_ch: 你怎么还在这儿当卧底…
t_en: Why are you still working undercover here?

t_ch: 嘘——小点声。
t_en: Shh! Keep your voice down.

t_ch: 上面的人说了，这建木虽然已经不再生发，乍看是危机解除了，但风波之后还有许多琐事要处理呢。
t_en: According to the higher-ups, there will be a lot of trivial matters to be handled even though the Ambrosial Arbor has stopped growing and the crisis is over.

t_ch: 我还得继续守着店铺，继续开张…时刻注意那些危机的苗头是否复燃……
t_en: I have to stay here and keep the store open... and watch out for signs of the crisis making a comeback...

t_ch: 哎，他们都说我有卧底经验，让我继续待几年看看。我就知道，迟早会有这么一天的。不知道何时才能再回我云骑军中去……
t_en: *Sigh* They want me to stay here for a couple more years because I've got undercover experience. I've seen this coming. When will I be able to return to the Cloud Knights...

t_ch: 十王司在此主理公务，无关人等一律回避。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission are handling official matters here. If this does not concern you, please leave.

t_ch: 你在这里主理什么公务？
t_en: What official matters are you handling here?

t_ch: 主理一些阁下不该知道的公务。
t_en: Matters that you shouldn't know about.

t_ch: 阁下要是有什么想知道，可以去问问那边在站岗的云骑队长，叫德威。他不知道的，差不多就是阁下不该知道的。
t_en: If you'd like to ask questions, please ask the Cloud Knight captain on duty over there. His name's Dewei. Anything he doesn't know is what you should not know about.

t_ch: 我算有关人士。
t_en: I am a concerned party.

t_ch: 有关人士？阁下算十王司的有关人士？有意思，在下怎么没听说最近有特赦啊？
t_en: A concerned party? Concerned with matters of the Ten-Lords Commission? How curious. I didn't hear about any amnesties recently?

t_ch: 现在这个情势下，十王司在鳞渊境的「有关人士」只有三种，判官、冥差、罪人。在下希望您不是最后一种。
t_en: At this present situation, only three types of "concerned parties" remain for the Ten-Lords Commission in Scalegorge Waterscape: Judge, spiritfarer, and criminal. I hope you do not fit into the final category.

t_ch: 阁下是域外来的贵客，机缘巧合下也成了景元的特使，罗浮上上下下都会给足您面子。
t_en: You are a distinguished guest from beyond the sky who became a special envoy to General Jing Yuan by sheer serendipity. You'll be accorded dignity all over the Luofu.

t_ch: 但云骑将军不过是「阳世」威权罢了，十王司统管长生种生死、罪咎、魔阴身等诸般事宜，不受其节制，论地位吾等更在其上。
t_en: But the Cloud Knights are merely the authority on matters of the mortal world. The Ten-Lords Commission governs various long-life species' concerns such as death, sins, being mara-struck, and so on. We are unrestricted by the Knights' authority, and are certainly above them in terms of status.

t_ch: 在这样的敏感时期，在下建议您恪守本分，不做任何越轨之举。在十王司这里，阁下没有任何特权。
t_en: During such sensitive times, I humbly suggest that you stay within your lane and refrain from stepping out of line. You enjoy no special privilege here at the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 阁下还有什么事吗？
t_en: Is there anything else I can do for you?

t_ch: 想问你点事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: …唉，阁下真是求知若渴。您问吧，请尽量问一些在下能回答的。
t_en: ...Ah, your thirst for knowledge is unquenchable. Please ask away and I'll answer what I'm able to.

t_ch: 你是做什么工作的？
t_en: What do you do for a living?

t_ch: 在下是十王司的冥差。平日里接引大限将至的仙舟住民入因果殿、归于寂灭。
t_en: I'm a humble spiritfarer for the Ten-Lords Commission. I receive everyday Xianzhou citizens into the Hall of Karma as they approach the end of their life and wait for their time to come.

t_ch: 若有人触犯了「不赦十恶」的条律，吾等就要随判官出巡罚罪。
t_en: If someone breaks any of the Ten Unpardonable Sins, then we accompany the judge outside of the Commission to investigate and carry out the appropriate disciplinary procedures.

t_ch: 你是个刑警？
t_en: Are you a police detective?

t_ch: 「刑警」？莫要拿短生种的事务生搬硬套…我们对付的东西可危险得多。
t_en: "Police"? "Detective"? Please don't conflate us with the customs of short-life species... The things we handle are far more dangerous.

t_ch: 小孩子干这行？
t_en: They let kids do this?

t_ch: 垂髫未必真童子…你来仙舟时间也不短了，怎地还不明白，在这儿「以貌取人」是最要不得的？
t_en: Don't let my appearance fool you... You have been aboard the Xianzhou long enough to understand that we do not judge others by their appearance around here.

t_ch: 十王司是做什么的？
t_en: What does the Ten-Lords Commission do?

t_ch: 我说过了，十王司统管长生种生死、罪咎、魔阴身等诸般事宜，不受六御节制。
t_en: As I said before, the Ten-Lords Commission governs various long-life species' concerns such as death, sins, being mara-struck, and so on. We are not restricted by the Six Charioteers.

t_ch: 在下知道这答案不能让您满意，但阁下还是别追问了。这个话题就到此为止吧。
t_en: I know that this answer doesn't satisfy your curiosity, but you really must cease this line of questioning. This topic is closed for discussion.

t_ch: 你在鳞渊境做什么？
t_en: What are you doing in Scalegorge Waterscape?

t_ch: 肃清鳞渊境内的药王秘传残党。阁下要是有什么线索，随时可以提供给在下。阁下要是有什么疑问…那还是请您去问德威吧。
t_en: Eliminating the remnant Disciples of Sanctus Medicus here. If you have any leads, please let me know at any time. And if you have any further questions... Then please direct those to Dewei.

t_ch: 还是那句话——德威不知道的，差不多就是阁下不该知道的。
t_en: As I always say: Anything Dewei doesn't know is what you should not know.

t_ch: 没什么事了。
t_en: I have nothing else.

t_ch: 好的，再见。当下时局尚不安定，请阁下务必注意安全。
t_en: That's good. So long. The current situation is not a stable one, so please take precautions.

t_ch: 没什么事了。
t_en: I have nothing else.

t_ch: 好的，再见。当下时局尚不安定，请阁下务必注意安全。
t_en: That's good. So long. The current situation is not a stable one, so please take precautions.

t_ch: 唉，我与同僚守护了二百年鳞渊境，到头来还是目睹着它乱成一片……
t_en: *sigh* My team and I have guarded Scalegorge Waterscape for over two hundred years, but we still have to witness it crumble to pieces in the end...

t_ch: 不知云骑的队伍几时能撤出此地，还这儿一片清净！
t_en: When will the Cloud Knights withdraw from this place and restore its sanctity!?

t_ch: 你看起来心情不太好。
t_en: You don't seem to be in the best mood?

t_ch: 是相当不好。话又说回来了，又有哪个持明目睹圣地遭难，心情会好呢？
t_en: That's putting it lightly. But then again, which Vidyadhara would be in good spirits after witnessing the desecration of our holy site?

t_ch: 你在碎碎念什么呢？
t_en: What are you muttering about?

t_ch: 你是…来丹鼎司求药的化外民？你来晚啦。这儿如今求不到治病的灵药，只有看着扎眼的云骑。
t_en: You are... an Outworlder seeking medicine from the Alchemy Commission? You're too late. There's no cure to be found at all anymore, only those Cloud Knights sticking out like a sore thumb.

t_ch: 哼，先是与丰饶民的血战，随后「饮月之乱」几乎动摇了「建木」根柢…如今「药王秘传」的小丑妖人又将这里搅成了一锅粥！
t_en: Hmph, first there was the bloody conflict against the Denizens of Abundance. Then the Sedition of Imbibitor Lunae almost shook the Ambrosial Arbor to its core... And now those clowns from the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus have turned everything into a steaming hot mess!

t_ch: 从军的时候我发誓保卫联盟，退伍返乡我成了「护珠人」，发誓护卫同胞蜕生转世。事到如今回头看，我到底保卫了什么呢？真是…寿多则辱。
t_en: When I signed up with the armed forces, I took an oath to defend the Alliance. After retiring I became a Pearlkeeper, vowing to protect my compatriots during their hatching rebirth. But looking back, what did I actually protect? Truly... longevity is a curse.

t_ch: 嗐，你瞧我，一开始诉苦就刹不住车。这人一上了岁数，话就变多了，你多包涵。你是有什么事情要找我吗？
t_en: Hey, look at me — once I start complaining, I can't stop. I've grown far too talkative in my old age, so please forgive me. Do you have anything that you'd like to ask me about?

t_ch: 想和你聊聊天。
t_en: I'd like to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 行啊。我曾在此处当了两百多年的护珠人。对这里熟得不能再熟了，你有什么想问的都可以问我。
t_en: Alright. I've been a Pearlkeeper here for over two hundred years. I know everything there is to know about this place, so you can ask me anything you want.

t_ch: 鳞渊境是什么地方？
t_en: What kind of place is Scalegorge Waterscape?

t_ch: 你眺目远望所看到的一切，在不可胜数的往昔，都曾是持明故土的一部分。但随着持明迁徙，我们将这故土移栽至了仙舟洞天中。
t_en: Everything you see before you has been part of the Vidyadhara homeworld for eons. But as we migrated, we've transplanted parts of our homeworld to some of the Xianzhou Delves.

t_ch: 鳞渊境便是其一，其中承载的古海之水，与罗浮持明的血脉紧密相连。无论是龙师长老、或是持明黎庶，都会在寿尽之日，踏入海中化为持明卵蜕生。
t_en: One of which is Scalegorge Waterscape, and the waters of the ancient sea it sustains are intimately connected to the blood of the Vidyadhara aboard the Luofu. Be they the Preceptors or the regular Vidyadhara folk, all will step into the waters at their end to be transformed into Vidyadhara eggs and undergo their hatching rebirth.

t_ch: 你现在明白它在我们心目中的地位了？
t_en: Do you understand now the significant of this place in our hearts?

t_ch: 可惜，药王秘传作乱后，云骑入驻此处，以「周护安全」为名，替代了护珠人的巡视。唉，我们虽百般不满，却也无用。
t_en: It's a shame that the Cloud Knights moved in after the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus rebelled. These Knights replaced our Pearlkeeper patrols in the name of "keeping the peace." Alas, our myriad of complaints amounted to naught.

t_ch: 听说「建木」重生是因为有人将星核送进了鳞渊境深处…哼，想来持明族中有人也被药王秘传的妖人蛊惑了。要不了多久，神策府也许会对龙师们发难吧？
t_en: I heard that the Arbor's resurrection was caused by someone plunging a Stellaron deep into the Scalegorge Waterscape waters... Hmph, thinking things through, some Vidyadhara may also have been seduced by those Disciple devils. It won't be long before the Seat of Divine Foresight comes after the Preceptors, will it?

t_ch: 护珠人是做什么的？
t_en: What do Pearlkeepers do?

t_ch: 护珠人啊…这可是个古老的行当。持明族蜕生成卵时，除了珠壳之外，对外界的危险毫无抵抗力。因此持明内部历来会赋予在世的青壮「护珠」的职责。
t_en: Pearlkeepers are... an ancient profession. When Vidyadhara molt into eggs, they have no protection from the outside world other than their eggshells. That's why the Vidyadhara have historically bestowed the role of Pearlkeeping upon their young and strong.

t_ch: 驾驭星槎，逐潮来去，检视那些珍珠般的持明卵，看着他们在沉睡中发光，真是世间最美妙的景象了。
t_en: It's the most wondrous sight in the cosmos to ride across the tides on a starskiff, marveling at those pearly Vidyadhara eggs as they glow in hibernation.

t_ch: 说起来，仙舟「方壶」的护珠人与我们有些不尽相同。「方壶」龙尊统御万民，六司六御都是辅佐龙尊的存在。
t_en: Speaking of which, the Pearlkeepers aboard the Xianzhou Fanghu are different from us. The Fanghu's high elder governs over all the citizens, and the Six Charioteers of the Six Commissions all support the high elder.

t_ch: 因而在仙舟「方壶」，护珠人基本上代替了云骑的岗位，是那里的精锐部队。
t_en: That's why the Pearlkeepers aboard the Xianzhou Fanghu formed an elite force that have basically replaced the role of Cloud Knights there.

t_ch: 很久以前，我在机缘巧合之下遇到过一位「方壶」护珠人，叫做粼汐。她的云吟术出神入化，几乎到了艺术的境地。
t_en: A long time ago, I had the pleasure of meeting a Pearlkeeper from the Xianzhou Fanghu named Linxi. Her cloudhymn magic was superb, almost transcending into the realm of artistry.

t_ch: 唉…如果那天她在鳞渊境，也许持明能少受不少损失吧…只可惜，很多事情都容不得「如果」……
t_en: Alas... If she'd been at Scalegorge Waterscape on that day, perhaps the Vidyadhara wouldn't have suffered such terrible losses... It's a real shame how many "what ifs" there are out there...

t_ch: 鳞渊境现在怎么样？
t_en: How is Scalegorge Waterscape these days?

t_ch: 我最近刚退役。听闻此次动乱中，有不少持明卵被打破…还有不少兄弟姐妹被药王秘传的妖人所害…可恶！
t_en: I've only just retired. I heard that during all the turmoil, many Vidyadhara eggs got cracked... Many of my brothers and sisters got harmed by those devils of Sanctus Medicus... Those hateful scoundrels!

t_ch: 唉，持明族无父无母，无有繁衍，永远在蜕生轮回中循环。每一个持明的死去，每一颗持明卵的损毁，将是整个持明族永远的损失。
t_en: Alas, we Vidyadhara have no parents, no reproductive process, and are forever stuck in the cycle of our hatching rebirth. Every death and every destruction of a Vidyadhara egg is an eternal loss for our species.

t_ch: 「建木」重生那天，我听到了消息，拿起潮信叉——喔，那是咱们护珠人的武器，回到了鳞渊境，想提供些力所能及的帮助。
t_en: The day I heard the news about the Arbor's resurrection, I picked up my tidal trident — the weapon that we Pearlkeepers use — and ran back to Scalegorge Waterscape to try and give as much support as I could.

t_ch: 当我赶到时，我目击三个药王秘传的妖人正在围攻一名落单的云骑，那三个叛匪竟然都是我的同族，只是外形生就了令人憎恶的变异……
t_en: When I got back, I witnessed three Sanctus Medicus devils surrounding a lone Cloud Knight. The three disciples were fellows of my own race, but had somehow been mutated into horrific forms...

t_ch: 那三人以伸出的利爪刺入云骑身体里，仿佛汲走了他的生命，他的脸一瞬间变得灰败苍老。
t_en: The three of them stabbed into the knight with their claws. And as if sucking away his life, the knight's face instantly grew old and gray.

t_ch: 我不敢犹豫，于是我…我动手了。我用云吟术指挥武器，将那三人…将那三个人击倒在地。他们再也无法蜕生了。
t_en: I didn't dare hesitate, so I... I took action. I used cloudhymn magic to direct my weapon at those three and I... I struck them all down. They won't have another hatching rebirth ever again.

t_ch: 族规中，染同胞血者当同受入灭永罚。我不知道我自己该受罚，还是能得到豁免？
t_en: Our people's creed states that those stained with the blood of a fellow Vidyadhara are punished for all eternity. I don't know whether I should be punished, or whether I could earn an exemption?

t_ch: 这段时间我一直在想，一直在想，但怎么想也想不通——我那时真的应该出手吗……
t_en: These days I can't stop thinking about this all the time, but I can never get over it. That day, did I really have to take up arms and...?

t_ch: 先不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll stop bothering you for now.

t_ch: 没事，不打扰，不打扰。要是想聊聊天，你可以随时来找我。我平时也找不到谁能跟我聊这些。
t_en: All good, it's no bother at all. Any time you want to chat, just come and find me. I usually can't find anyone to talk with about this stuff.

t_ch: 先不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll stop bothering you for now.

t_ch: 没事，不打扰，不打扰。要是想聊聊天，你可以随时来找我。我平时也找不到谁能跟我聊这些。
t_en: All good, it's no bother at all. Any time you want to chat, just come and find me. I usually can't find anyone to talk with about this stuff.

t_ch: 愁啊，愁。那首持明时调怎么唱的来着…「六百余年凡尘中，如梦尽是空」，哈哈哈哈，妙哉，妙哉。
t_en: How sad. How sad! How does that Vidyadhara elegy go again... "Pass like a dream out of sight and mind, six hundred years in this mortal bind"... Ha, simply wonderful.

t_ch: 嗯？{NICKNAME}？今天怎么有空来这么个萧索的地方晃悠啊？是有什么事要办吗？
t_en: Mmmm, {NICKNAME}? How'd you find the time to come hang out at this wasteland? Anything going on?

t_ch: 想和你聊聊天。
t_en: I'd like to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 行啊。我退休以前，在这鳞渊境也当了二百多年的护珠人了。对这里熟得不能再熟了，你有什么想问的都可以问我。
t_en: Alright. Before I retired, I was a Pearlkeeper here for over two hundred years. I know everything there is to know about this place, so you can ask me anything you want.

t_ch: 鳞渊境是什么地方？
t_en: What kind of place is Scalegorge Waterscape?

t_ch: 你眺目远望所看到的一切，在不可胜数的往昔，都曾是持明故土的一部分。但随着持明迁徙，我们将这故土移栽至了仙舟洞天中。

t_ch: 族规中，染同胞血者当同受入灭永罚。我不知道我自己该受罚，还是我做对了？
t_en: Our people's creed states that those stained with the blood of a fellow Vidyadhara are punished for all eternity. I don't know whether I should be punished, or whether I was right?

t_ch: 这段时间我一直在想，一直在想，但怎么想也想不通——我那时真的应该那么做吗……
t_en: These days I can't stop thinking about this all the time, but I can never get over it. That day, did I really have to do that...

t_ch: 先不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll stop bothering you for now.

t_ch: 没事，不打扰，不打扰。要是想聊聊天，你可以随时来找我。我平时也找不到谁能跟我聊这些。
t_en: All good, it's no bother at all. Any time you want to chat, just come and find me. I usually can't find anyone to talk with about this stuff.

t_ch: 先不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll stop bothering you for now.

t_ch: 没事，不打扰，不打扰。要是想聊聊天，你可以随时来找我。我平时也找不到谁能跟我聊这些。
t_en: All good, it's no bother at all. Any time you want to chat, just come and find me. I usually can't find anyone to talk with about this stuff.

t_ch: 海滩守护者可真不好做啊…
t_en: Life as a guardian of the shoreline is tough...

t_ch: 是啊！我委托你做的海岸巡逻，甚至还只是护珠人平时工作的一小部分呢。
t_en: Indeed! The coast patrolling task that I entrusted to you is actually just a small part of a Pearlkeeper's duties.

t_ch: 其实社会上一直有一些不和谐的声音，说护珠人工作轻松，是持明族的「养老闲职」…但现在你也亲身体会过了，天底下没有这么累的闲职。
t_en: In fact, many in society regard the Pearlkeepers contemptuously, assuming they're the Vidyadhara equivalent of an easy job you can retire in... Now that you've experienced it yourself, I bet you know the job is not the least bit easy.

t_ch: 以后你要遇到有人和你说这种话，还请你帮我狠狠教训那个人——当然，要用语言教训，不要用拳头。
t_en: If you meet anyone who says things like that to you, please teach that person a lesson for me. Of course, you should do it verbally, not physically.

t_ch: 蜕生陪伴服务恢复运营！「百般冗物，一抛成空；旧缘宿怨，舍手烟消。」
t_en: Normal resumption of hatching rebirth assistance services! "Dispose of all your troubles, leave old grudges in the dust."

t_ch: 哎哟，这位客官，我看您不是持明族人，应该不需要蜕生陪伴服务吧？
t_en: Hello there, dear customer. You don't look like a Vidyadhara, so I'm guessing you don't require any hatching rebirth assistance?

t_ch: 莫非您是来丹鼎司采购药材的？我这里也有地道的鳞渊境药材，你想要什么？鲛人泪，还是鳞渊天冬？龙鳞珊瑚，还是苍茯苓？
t_en: Could it be that you came to the Alchemy Commission to buy some medicine? I've got some genuine Scalegorge Waterscape medicine with me. What do you need? Draconic Tears, or perhaps Tian Dong? Dragon scale coral, or cyan poria maybe?

t_ch: 你到底是做什么生意的？
t_en: What exactly do you do?

t_ch: 兼职这种事，应该很常见吧？
t_en: Never seen someone work part-time jobs before?

t_ch: 我是驻扎在丹鼎司洞天，专为持明族提供蜕生陪伴业务的管理者。凡是族内有自觉行将转世的同胞，向龙师们禀报后，在各处浮岛上找到像我这样的人，就能准备入海蜕生。
t_en: I'm the manager assigned to the Alchemy Commission delve to provide hatching rebirth assistance for the Vidyadhara. Whenever one of our race volunteers to undergo hatching rebirth, they can report to the Preceptors before coming to someone like me on the floating isles, then get ready to submerge into the waters for hatching rebirth.

t_ch: 干这行虽然稳定，但也没太多收入。所以我也兼干些药材生意。毕竟，丹鼎司平日也有不少商客造访采买药物。
t_en: It's a steady job, but it doesn't pay much. So I also sling a little medicine on the side. You know how it is. The Alchemy Commission will usually have merchants visiting who are keen on purchasing medicines.

t_ch: 你刚刚念的口号又是？
t_en: What's that slogan you just said?

t_ch: 是说「百般冗物，一抛成空；旧缘宿业，舍手烟消。」…这一句么？
t_en: You mean "Dispose of all your troubles, leave old grudges in the dust"?

t_ch: 这是我自个儿编的。不过按照习俗，持明蜕生便是将前世种种一笔勾销了。可说来也不奇怪，来这儿准备入海的人身上却总会带着一两件无法割舍的东西。
t_en: I just made that up myself. But our Vidyadhara tradition involves casting off everything from their previous lives. It's hardly a surprise that those submerging here may be carrying a few things with them that are difficult to let go of.

t_ch: 我会允许他们把这些东西暂时寄放在我这儿。
t_en: I let them stow items with me while the rebirth happens.

t_ch: 看您也不像是来买药的。不知道客官对这些持明蜕生留下的东西感兴趣吗？便宜的很！咱就收一个仓储费用。
t_en: I don't think you're here to buy any medicine. I wonder if you're interested in some of the stuff left behind by these hatching rebirth Vidyadhara? I'll cut you a deal! Just one low storage fee.

t_ch: 既然是寄放，你怎能卖了？
t_en: How can you sell the stuff if they're just asking you to hold onto it?

t_ch: 这您就有所不知了。族人蜕生前累积的财产要不是分赠了，便是归公了。
t_en: Ah, so you don't know about the Vidyadhara way. We give away all of our worldly possessions before being reborn. Anything left over is requisitioned by the state.

t_ch: 虽说这儿放着他们「割舍不下的东西」，但都是些旧情难忘的小物件：什么定情的挂坠、老友的书信、或是自己的作品……
t_en: Even though it's all stuff people "struggle to let go of," it's really all just small sentimental trinkets: Engraved love pendants, letters from old friends, or even works they've personally created...

t_ch: 唉，上一世中觉得无比重要的东西，在古海浣洗记忆、结卵新生后，就再也不重要了。这些遗物无一例外，没有哪个持明族在重生后曾来向我讨要。
t_en: *Sigh* But what was once of utmost importance becomes of zero relevance when this ancient sea cleanses away all memory and hatches new life. Without exception, no Vidyadhara has ever returned post-resurrection to ask for any of these relics back.

t_ch: 到头来，它们就成了压箱底的旧货。咱就只好勉为其难，定时把它们卖出去了嘛。
t_en: So really they're just out-of-fashion goods consigned to the bottom of the heap. I have no choice but to sell them off regularly.

t_ch: 这些私人物品的「价值」都是人生经历赋予的…所以客人您也不必在意，就大大方方地挑选好了。
t_en: The "value" of these personal items is conferred by life experiences... So you don't really have to give it much thought. Just pick out anything you like.

t_ch: 正好，拿给我看看。
t_en: Okay, let me have a look.

t_ch: 成嘞，咱就喜欢好说话的客人。
t_en: Alright! I love customers who are open to new things.

t_ch: 客官，您随便看看，有什么需求随时叫我好吧。
t_en: Dear customer, take a gander around and call me over if you need anything at all.

t_ch: 让我看看你这有什么？
t_en: Let me see what you have.

t_ch: 保证物美价廉。
t_en: I guarantee you high quality at a low price.

t_ch: 能聊聊天吗？
t_en: Can we chat for a bit?

t_ch: 行呀，客人要是不嫌弃，咱们多聊一会儿也行。不过，要是有其他客人来了的话，我们俩的闲谈就停下吧。
t_en: Okay, we can keep chatting on if you wish. However, if another customer comes along, I'll have to attend to them.

t_ch: 蜕生的人都留了什么东西？
t_en: What do people leave behind when they undergo hatching rebirth?

t_ch: 大多数人蜕生前都不愿放手的东西，多半跟他们自己的人生经历有关。都是些旧情难忘的小物件：什么定情的挂坠、老友的书信、或是自己的作品……
t_en: Most things people are unwilling to part ways with are connected to experiences from their life. It's really all just small sentimental trinkets: Engraved love pendants, letters from old friends, or even works they've personally created...

t_ch: 还有些人会给来生留下一封信，祝自己的来生一帆风顺，不要再误入前尘往事之类的。期许本身是好的，但用处嘛…不能说有效，只能说百无一用。
t_en: Some people even leave a letter behind for their future self, wishing them all the best and imploring them not to commit the same mistakes they did. The intention is good, but as for its usefulness... It is practically useless.

t_ch: 转世重生，「我」早已不是当年的「我」了。谁又会在意上一世的活法和建议？倒不如留个值钱的坠子，也好利益同胞嘛~
t_en: After rebirth, "you" are not the same "you" as before. Who cares about how your previous life was lived, or what suggestions might get passed on? You're better off bequeathing a valuable pendant or whatever for the future benefit of your fellows!

t_ch: 你真是打得好算盘。
t_en: You really do have a cunning scheme.

t_ch: 客官，话不可以这样说呀。别人不要了，我这里才会收下嘛。
t_en: Please don't say that, my dear customer. I only accept things that people don't want.

t_ch: 蜕生后就是新的「我」？
t_en: So after hatching rebirth you're a new "you"?

t_ch: 没错，据我观察，刚蜕生不久的持明族大多缺乏感情，也理解不了前世的做法。能从这些旧物中辨识出自己寄存之物的，一个也没有。
t_en: Exactly. From what I've seen, most Vidyadhara lack emotions fresh after their rebirth, and are unable to understand the sentiments of their previous selves. Not a single one has ever been able to identify which one of these items used to belong to them.

t_ch: 转念一想，这就是持明轮回的神圣之处。龙祖赐予了我们肉体的永生，但也给了我们不受往日罪孽枷缚的机会。
t_en: On second thought, this is the reality of the sacred Vidyadhara reincarnation cycle. Long granted us eternal life of the flesh, but also blessed us with the opportunity to absolve ourselves from the sins of our previous lives.

t_ch: 要是一个人永远被同一个念头、同一个自我所困，那岂不是很可悲？
t_en: Wouldn't it be a tremendous tragedy if someone was eternally stuck with the same thoughts and the same ego?

t_ch: 鳞渊境的药材听上去很奇妙。
t_en: The medicines of Scalegorge Waterscape sound simply miraculous.

t_ch: 是吗？确实有很多化外民都觉得仙舟医学很奇妙。
t_en: Really? I guess lots of Outworlders usually do find Xianzhou medicine to be rather wonderful.

t_ch: 比如这些叶子、果实、树皮、根茎…它们大多是受「建木」力量波及所生，成分奇异，入药后更有神效。
t_en: Take these leaves, fruit, bark, and rhizomes for instance... They're miraculous ingredients, mostly evolved following exposure to the power of the Ambrosial Arbor. And they have even more potent effects once used to make medicines.

t_ch: 虽然咱也不是丹鼎司的医士，说不出个一二三四五。但是咱听说，这些药材在仙舟外也大受欢迎，不知道是真的假的。
t_en: I'm no Alchemy Commission healer or anything and barely know the basics. But still, I hear this medicine is really popular beyond Xianzhou. Though I can't vouch for whether that's true or not.

t_ch: 这次就这样吧。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 欢迎客官您下次再来。
t_en: I warmly invite you to visit again next time.

t_ch: 这次就这样吧。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 欢迎客官您下次再来。
t_en: I warmly invite you to visit again next time.

t_ch: 客官您又来了，最近有蜕生的客人留下了些好东西，您赏脸看看吧。
t_en: You're back. We've had some hatching rebirth customers leave behind a few more items recently. Please have a good look.

t_en: Let me see what you have.

t_en: Take these leaves, fruit, bark, and rhizomes for instance... Most are peculiar flora from the Ambrosial Arbor's time, and their effects are difficult for standard metrics to analyze. They just work like some kind of miracle.

t_ch: 这次就这样吧。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 欢迎客官您下次再来。
t_en: I warmly invite you to visit again next time.

t_ch: 这次就这样吧。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 欢迎客官您下次再来。
t_en: I warmly invite you to visit again next time.

t_ch: 啊啊，我受不了了…这样的日子什么时候是个头啊……
t_en: Ahhh, I can't take it anymore! When will this end...

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you all right?

t_ch: 多谢您关心，但我实在是不太好……
t_en: Thank you for checking in. I'm really not doing too well...

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 我怎么了…唉，这可就说来话长了……
t_en: What's wrong? Well... It's a long story.

t_ch: 几个月前，我联系不上自己的最喜欢的徒弟了。最近才听说，她加入了药王秘传。
t_en: A few months back, I lost touch with my favorite apprentice. But I've just heard that she's been sucked into the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus.

t_ch: 后来，我听说全城都在搜捕我们丹士长。那时我才发现，丹枢大人加入了药王秘传。
t_en: Later, I learned that the whole city was hunting down our chief alchemist. Only then did I realize that Lady Dan Shu had joined up with the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus.

t_ch: 前段时间，我出差走了一段时间，回来发现丹鼎司被查封了。一问才知道，我们这有不少人加入了药王秘传。
t_en: I went away for business a while ago, and I came back to find that the Alchemy Commission had been sealed off for investigation. I was told that many of us had been found to be colluding with the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus.

t_ch: 昨天晚上，我点了一份刺毛猪脚饭，饭馆说现在没办法送外卖到丹鼎司。我猜想，是因为猪脚饭也加入了药王秘传吧。
t_en: Just last night, I ordered some razorboar trotter rice, but the diner said they couldn't deliver it to the Alchemy Commission anymore. I'm guessing it's because even the trotter rice has joined up with the Disciples.

t_ch: 够了，真是够了，我真*仙舟粗口*受够了。
t_en: Enough is enough. This is *Xianzhou profanity* enough!!

t_ch: 哎…算了，我也不跟您释放负面情绪了…我才看出来，您不是星穹列车上的贵客吗？刚才失礼了…您有什么事吗？
t_en: Ah... Forget it. I don't want to just dump my traumas all over you... Ha, I've only just realized, aren't you our esteemed guest from the Astral Express? Please forgive my rudeness just now... Is there anything I can do for you?

t_ch: 有些事想问你。
t_en: I have a few questions I wish to ask you...

t_ch: 有事您尽管问。虽然我也刚回本部没多久，未必能答上来就是了。
t_en: Ask away, though I've just returned to the headquarters and might not have all the answers.

t_ch: 你看起来心情不太好？
t_en: You don't seem to be in a good mood.

t_ch: 心情确实不太好。这该从何说起呢…唉…我从头给您讲吧。
t_en: Indeed. I'm not feeling too good. How should I explain this? Hmm... let me start from the beginning.

t_ch: 我基本上算是在这丹鼎司长起来的。我小时候，周围的大人们就说，这丹鼎司是要衰败了。
t_en: I kind of grew up in the Alchemy Commission. When I was little, the adults around me kept saying that the commission was going to decline.

t_ch: 结果过了二百多年，我都做了医士了，这丹鼎司还是涛声依旧，看上去没什么衰败的苗头。
t_en: Two hundred years later, I became a healer, and the Alchemy Commission was still busy as ever, showing no signs of going downhill.

t_ch: 再之后啊，司里组织了学术交流团，让一批罗浮丹鼎司的学者出去和域外的学者们交流经验。
t_en: Later, the commission organized an academic exchange program. A batch of Luofu scholars would be sent off to interact with Outworld scholars.

t_ch: 当时领导可是允诺的，在学术交流团里混够了资历，回来高低能评上个医士长。所以我就跟着去了。
t_en: At that time, the leader promised that whoever gained enough experience in the academic exchange program would very likely be promoted to chief healer. So, I joined the program.

t_ch: 我在方壶仙舟交流了一百五十多年，又在博识学会的哈米特空间站交流了一百五十多年，最后在公司的法特森星际医院交流了一百多年……
t_en: I spent about <unbreak>150</unbreak> years on the Xianzhou Fanghu, and another <unbreak>150</unbreak> years at the Hamet Space Station of the Intelligentsia Guild. And finally, I was at IPC's Fedson Interstellar Hospital for over <unbreak>100</unbreak> years...

t_ch: 最后我终于混够了资历，发够了论文，回到丹鼎司总部等着升迁。结果还没过几个月呢…您猜怎么着？丹鼎司造反啦！都加入那什么「药王秘传」啦！
t_en: After I had finally gained enough experience and published enough articles, I came back to headquarters, waiting to be promoted. Guess what happened just months after my return? The Alchemy Commission revolted and joined the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus!

t_ch: 到了这份上，我就别惦记着评医士长了…只希望抓那些药王秘传的时候可不要牵连到我……
t_en: At this point, I should forget about becoming the chief healer... I just hope that I won't be implicated when they're hunting down those Disciples of Sanctus Medicus...

t_ch: 你和那些药王秘传很熟吗？
t_en: Are you close with those Disciples of Sanctus Medicus?

t_ch: 您这问得我都不敢答了…怎么说呢，毕竟确实很多信徒都是丹鼎司人士，和其中一些…我还挺熟的……
t_en: I don't dare to answer such a question... After all, many of the disciples also worked at the Alchemy Commission. So, I'm quite close with some of them...

t_ch: 就比如说我那个徒弟…司里给她外派到法特森星际医院的时候，她才四十多岁。我带了她五十多年，后来她先回了罗浮，还一直和我有书信往来。
t_en: Take my apprentice as an example. She was only in her forties when she was sent to the Fedson Interstellar Hospital where I trained her for about fifty years. She returned to the Luofu before me and kept in touch with me through letters.

t_ch: 结果几个月前联系不上了…等我到了罗浮，去她家问她的下落，她家人也说，这孩子下落不明了。
t_en: I lost contact with her a few months ago... It was only when I was back at the Luofu and visited her home when her family told me that she had gone missing.

t_ch: 再后来就是听说她持械袭击云骑，让人家给当场击毙了。直到那时候，我才知道她走了邪路。
t_en: Later, I learned that she was killed during an armed attack against the Cloud Knights. Only then did I know that she had gone astray.

t_ch: 当时我是真不想继续干这个鬼差事了，真不想干了。难过，心里恨得慌。可是恨又能怎么样呢？我日哭夜哭，能哭死寿瘟祸祖吗？
t_en: At that time, I lost all my desire to stick with this lousy job. I was upset and overwhelmed by resentment. But what could I do? Could I destroy the Plagues Author by crying day and night?

t_ch: 这么一想，我觉得还是应当回到丹鼎司，继续做这个破医士。也许我什么都做不了，但总比什么都不做要强。
t_en: At that thought, I decided to remain a healer at the Alchemy Commission. I might not be able to accomplish anything huge, but it's better than nothing.

t_ch: 你多保重，再见。
t_en: Take care and goodbye.

t_ch: 唉…借您吉言吧。您也多保重……
t_en: *Sigh*... I appreciate your encouragement. Take care...

t_ch: 你多保重，再见。
t_en: Take care and goodbye.

t_ch: 唉…借您吉言吧。您也多保重……
t_en: *Sigh*... I appreciate your encouragement. Take care...

t_ch: 唉…接下来可怎么办才好啊…哎呀，您来了？今天有什么事吗？
t_en: Sigh... What should I do next... Hey! What brings you here today?

t_ch: 有些事想问你。
t_en: I have a few questions I wish to ask you...

t_ch: 有事您尽管问。虽然我也刚回本部没多久，未必能答上来就是了。
t_en: Ask away, though I've just returned to the headquarters and might not have all the answers.

t_en: You don't seem to be in a good mood.

t_ch: 这么一想，我觉得还是应当回到丹鼎司，继续做这个破医士。也许我什么都做不了，但总比什么都不做要强。
t_en: At that thought, I decided to remain a healer at the Alchemy Commission. I might not be able to accomplish anything huge, but it's better than nothing.

t_ch: 你多保重，再见。
t_en: Take care and goodbye.

t_ch: 唉…借您吉言吧。您也多保重……
t_en: *Sigh*... I appreciate your encouragement. Take care...

t_ch: 你多保重，再见。
t_en: Take care and goodbye.

t_ch: 唉…借您吉言吧。您也多保重……
t_en: *Sigh*... I appreciate your encouragement. Take care...

t_ch: 这到底是怎么回事啊，怎么就乱成这样啦？
t_en: What in the world is going on? Why are things in such a mess?

t_ch: 一回来连个认识的人都没见到，就莫名其妙地升职成丹士长了，哎……
t_en: I didn't even get to meet anyone I knew when I got back, and I just got promoted to chief alchemist all of a sudden. *Sigh*...

t_ch: 那我恭喜你啊。
t_en: Congratulations!

t_ch: 谢谢…哎……
t_en: Thanks... *Sigh*...

t_ch: 这到底是怎么回事啊？
t_en: What exactly is going on?

t_ch: …你问我？我、我也不知道啊！
t_en: ...Are you asking me? I—I have no idea too!

t_ch: 等等，你别学我说话。
t_en: Hang on. Forget what I said.

t_ch: 咦，你来这里是有什么事吗？
t_en: Hey, what brings you here?

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 那个，我得先说清楚——我才回罗浮不久，对这里的一切还不太熟，你要是想问路或者打听什么，我未必能帮到你，到时候你可别怪我。
t_en: Just so you know, I've only been back for a short while, and I'm not very familiar with things around here in Luofu. If you want to ask for directions or about the latest happenings, I might not be very helpful, just so you know.

t_ch: 我听说药王秘传残党在闹事？
t_en: I heard that the remnants of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus are causing trouble here.

t_ch: 哎，在回来的路上我就听说啦，没想到你也知道啊？
t_en: Oh, I heard about that on my way back. I'm surprised you know about it too.

t_ch: 说是有这么回事，然后云骑军封锁了部分地区，正在缉拿乱党呢。
t_en: It seems to be true. The Cloud Knights have sealed off some areas and are hunting them down.

t_ch: 但具体是怎么闹起来的，这些事我倒是不知道了……
t_en: Still, I have no idea about the details...

t_ch: 你怎么就成为丹士长了？
t_en: How did you become the chief alchemist?

t_ch: 我也不知道啊，我本来在外面出差呢，医术研讨会还没开完，就突然收到消息说我升职了。
t_en: I have no clue. I was participating in a medical conference outside the Luofu when I was suddenly notified of the promotion via a message.

t_ch: 收到消息之后，这会我都开不下去了，赶紧收拾行李回来交接，结果一回来，就连安排交接的人都见不着。
t_en: I left the conference halfway and quickly packed my bags. When I got back, not a single soul was there to arrange the handover.

t_ch: 说是出了什么大事，云骑军正在排查，一切事项都得延后，哎，我就被晾在这儿了。
t_en: I was told that something huge had happened, and the Cloud Knights were doing an investigation, so everything had to be put off. So, I'm left here, clueless.

t_ch: 有什么内幕消息吗？
t_en: Do you have the lowdown on the situation?

t_ch: 啊？「内幕消息」？又出什么事了？我怎么什么动静都没听到。
t_en: Huh? Lowdown? What happened again? How come I didn't hear anything?

t_ch: 到底什么事啊？呜呜，为什么都没人告诉我——你要是知道的话记得第一时间跟我分享分享。
t_en: What on earth happened? *Sob* Why has no one told me about it? If you find anything, please share it with me as well.

t_ch: 你怎么什么都不知道！
t_en: How come you don't know anything?

t_ch: 我也不知道啊！啊，不对，我的确什么都不知道啊！
t_en: How should I know!? I really don't know anything!

t_ch: 我这才出差了多久，怎么感觉一回来整个世界都变了，这还是我认识的丹鼎司吗？ 到底发生什么事了……
t_en: I wasn't even away for that long! Why do I feel like the whole world has changed? Is this still the Alchemy Commission I know? What happened...?

t_ch: 算了，我没什么想问的了。
t_en: Forget it. I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 丹枢大人她真是…世事难料啊……
t_en: About Lady Dan Shu... Life truly is unpredictable...

t_ch: 算了，我没什么想问的了。
t_en: Forget it. I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 丹枢大人她真是…世事难料啊……
t_en: About Lady Dan Shu... Life truly is unpredictable...

t_ch: 一回来连个认识的人都没见到，就莫名其妙地升职成丹士长了，哎……
t_en: I didn't even get to meet anyone I knew when I got back, and I just got promoted to chief alchemist all of a sudden. *Sigh*...

t_ch: 哎，是你啊！怎么样，丹鼎司里有什么新消息吗？
t_en: Ah, it's you again. Anything new with the Alchemy Commission?

t_ch: 你身为丹士长还问我？
t_en: Why are you asking me? Aren't you the chief alchemist?

t_ch: 哎，我是丹士长又如何，连任命通知都是回来的路上突然收到的，至今都没搞清楚这儿发生什么事了，哎……
t_en: So what? I only received my appointment notice suddenly on my way back. I still can't make heads or tails of the situation here. *Sigh*...

t_ch: 呃，我只是路过。
t_en: Um, I was just passing by.

t_ch: 那也比我强，我才回罗浮不久，别说罗浮的近况了，就连丹鼎司上下的事，我也是一头雾水。
t_en: That's better than me. Since I've just gotten back, I know little about the Alchemy Commission's affairs, let alone the latest happenings on the Luofu.

t_ch: 好吧，那你找我是有什么事吗？
t_en: Now, what can I do for you?

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 那个，我得先说清楚——我才回罗浮不久，对这里的一切还不太熟，你要是想问路或者打听什么，我未必能帮到你，到时候你可别怪我。
t_en: Just so you know, I've only been back for a short while, and I'm not very familiar with things around here in Luofu. If you want to ask for directions or about the latest happenings, I might not be very helpful, just so you know.

t_ch: 我听说药王秘传残党在闹事？
t_en: I heard that the remnants of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus are causing trouble here.

t_ch: 哎，在回来的路上我就听说啦，没想到你也知道啊？
t_en: Oh, I heard about that on my way back. I'm surprised you know about it too.

t_ch: 说是有这么回事，然后云骑军封锁了部分地区，正在缉拿乱党呢。
t_en: It seems to be true. The Cloud Knights have sealed off some areas and are hunting them down.

t_ch: 但具体是怎么闹起来的，这些事我倒是不知道了……
t_en: Still, I have no idea about the details...

t_en: How did you become the chief alchemist?

t_en: I left the conference halfway and quickly packed my bags. When I got back, not a single soul was there to arrange the handover.

t_en: I was told that something huge had happened, and the Cloud Knights were doing an investigation, so everything had to be put off. So, I'm left here, clueless.

t_en: Do you have the lowdown on the situation?

t_en: Huh? Lowdown? What happened again? How come I didn't hear anything?

t_ch: 到底什么事啊？呜呜，为什么都没人告诉我——你要是知道的话记得第一时间跟我分享分享。
t_en: What on earth happened? *Sob* Why has no one told me about it? If you find anything, please share it with me as well.

t_ch: 你怎么什么都不知道！
t_en: How come you don't know anything?

t_ch: 我也不知道啊！啊，不对，我的确什么都不知道啊！
t_en: How should I know!? I really don't know anything!

t_ch: 我这才出差了多久，怎么感觉一回来整个世界都变了，这还是我认识的丹鼎司吗？ 到底发生什么事了……
t_en: I wasn't even away for that long! Why do I feel like the whole world has changed? Is this still the Alchemy Commission I know? What happened...?

t_ch: 算了，我没什么想问的了。
t_en: Forget it. I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 丹枢大人她真是…世事难料啊……
t_en: About Lady Dan Shu... Life truly is unpredictable...

t_ch: 算了，我没什么想问的了。
t_en: Forget it. I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 丹枢大人她真是…世事难料啊……
t_en: About Lady Dan Shu... Life truly is unpredictable...

t_ch: 丹士长做得还顺利吗…
t_en: How's your life as the chief alchemist?

t_ch: 开始勉强找到一点丹士长的感觉了…但还是不太行啊，我这人不适合当领导啊。
t_en: I'm more familiar with the job now, but I still don't think I can handle it. I'm just not cut out to be a leader.

t_ch: 啊，受不了了，每天唯一的想法就是想逃跑，想逃到外星球去……
t_en: Ugh, I just can't take it. I spend every day thinking about how to escape to another planet...

t_ch: 但是我还是不能逃走。你知道的，出了这样的事情之后，人们对丹鼎司的评价在不断下降，但是我知道大部分医士和丹士并非邪教徒，他们需要有人支持。
t_en: But I can't do it. As you know, the public image of the Alchemy Commission has been greatly undermined after what took place previously. I know most healers and alchemists aren't cultists, and they need my support.

t_ch: 虽然前路不易，丹鼎司定会重新建立起信任的。
t_en: Although the future is challenging, the Alchemy Commission will regain the people's trust.

t_ch: 我有种预感：今天一定能逃出去！
t_en: I have a premonition: Today is going to be the day I escape!

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}你来的正好，我即将成为第一个靠技巧逃出幽囚狱的人，帮我见证下这个伟大时刻。
t_en: You've come at the perfect time, {F#girl}{M#boy}! I am about to be the first person to ever escape The Shackling Prison using my skills alone. You must be my witness to this great moment!

t_ch: 我见证历史了？
t_en: I'm going to witness history?

t_ch: 要是有摄像机就好了，这样就可以把越狱全过程拍个纪录片，片名就叫《白鸟的救赎》，肯定卖爆。
t_en: If only we had a video camera, then I could record it and make it into a film called: Shiratori's Redemption! I just know it would go viral.

t_ch: 别白费力气了。
t_en: Don't waste your energy.

t_ch: 你和那些鼠目寸光的囚犯一样，只会说风凉话。等着瞧吧，我准备了无数个日夜，这次万无一失。
t_en: You're just like those narrow-minded prisoners, brushing me off and being sarcastic. Just you wait. I have been preparing for this for countless days and nights. Nothing can go wrong.

t_ch: 收手吧，外面全是金人武卒。
t_en: Just give up. The place is surrounded by aurumaton soldiers.

t_ch: 你不会相信那些金属脑袋能战胜我聪明的大脑吧？那些金人傻乎乎的，行动轨迹早被我摸透了。
t_en: You don't believe that those bolt-brains could ever compete with my great mind, do you? Those aurumatons are simple and dim-witted. I figured out their patrol routes a long time ago!

t_ch: 白鸟扑棱着翅膀一顿操作……
t_en: Shiratori begins to flap his wings and...

t_ch: 哎呀呀…又失败了，为什么会这样，这已经是第一千三百八十一次失败了。
t_en: Ow-ow-owww... I failed again? How? This is my <unbreak>1,381</unbreak>st failure...

t_ch: 呜呜呜…明明应该没问题了啊，到底是哪一步做错了？
t_en: Waaaa... I thought I had taken care of every problem! Where could I have gone wrong?

t_ch: 你没事吧…
t_en: Are you okay...?

t_ch: 啊哈哈哈…有趣，幽囚狱实在是太有趣了。这才是监狱该有的样子，相比之下我之前蹲过的那些监狱简直就是菜市场。
t_en: Ahaha... Interesting! The Shackling Prison truly is fascinating. This is what a real prison should be like. It makes all those other prisons I've served time in look like open-air markets!

t_ch: 我已经搞清楚问题出在哪了，只要稍微改良下方法。先这样，然后再那样…下次一定可以成功！
t_en: I have already ascertained where the problem was. I just need to slightly amend my approach... First I do this, then this, then this... I know I'll make it next time!

t_ch: 看来你没事。
t_en: Looks like you're okay.

t_ch: 我怎么可能有事呢，我现在好得不得了。一想到自己正在破解的是全银河最复杂的监狱，我浑身上下都充满了斗志。
t_en: Why would I not be okay? I've never been better! Just knowing that I am in the process of cracking the most complex prison in the entire cosmos fills my whole body with fighting spirit!

t_ch: 你这么明目张胆没人管么。
t_en: Does no one care that you're so obviously trying to escape?

t_ch: 那些狱卒早就见怪不怪了，有时候我都在怀疑他们是故意不管我的。
t_en: Those prison guards have seen everything. Sometimes I suspect they're deliberately leaving me to my own devices.

t_ch: 我和那些时刻想要搞破坏的危险份子不一样，我是技术流。除了扇翅膀的声音有点大，造不成什么危害。
t_en: I'm not like those dangerous criminals who are always trying to destroy stuff to escape. I prefer to use my skills. My wings may make a lot of noise when I flap them, but they can't cause any real damage.

t_ch: 会不会是十王司的圈套。
t_en: What if it's a trap set by the Ten-Lords Commission?

t_ch: 我才不管是不是圈套呢，只要让我有机关和谜题破解就行。不得不说十王司确实有两把刷子，每次都把我绕得七荤八素的。
t_en: I don't care if it's a trap. I'm happy as long as I have some mechanisms or puzzles to crack. I have to hand it to the Ten-Lords Commission, though. They really leave me scratching my head every time.

t_ch: 不过我现在有些累了，休息下再战吧。
t_en: I'm a little tired now though. I'll get some rest before trying again.

t_ch: 对了，{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}，我之前没见过你啊，你是刚进来的么。年纪轻轻就进了幽囚狱，可以啊你。
t_en: By the way, {F#girl}{M#boy}, I haven't seen you around before. You fresh round here? Getting put into The Shackling Prison at such a young age... That's some impressive work.

t_ch: 我来办事，刚好问你点问题…
t_en: I'm here for business. There are some things I wanted to ask you...

t_ch: 是说你看起来不像犯人。闲着也是闲着，就陪你聊聊吧。
t_en: I didn't think you looked like a criminal. Well, I've got nothing else to do. We may as well chat for a while.

t_ch: 你是犯了什么事被关进来？
t_en: What did you do to get locked up in here?

t_ch: 具体什么事…呃，我自己都记不清了，但我一不会偷二不会抢，所以肯定犯的不是大事。
t_en: Ah... I don't really, uhhh, remember. But I'm not a robber or a thief, so it can't have been anything serious.

t_ch: 但我这人吧，进了局子就不老实。刚开始他们把我关进地衡司的治安所，那里实在是太无聊了，我没忍住就跑了出来。
t_en: But I just can't sit still. It was when they locked me up that I started misbehaving. At first, they had me locked up in the Realm-Keeping Commission penitentiary, but I was bored to tears, so I had to escape.

t_ch: 这不就喜提越狱罪了么，他们便给我找了看守更严密的监狱。但那地方也没有趣多少，我一没忍住又跑了出来。
t_en: Obviously, that got me arrested for jailbreaking, so they got a prison with tighter security for me. But that place was just as boring. I couldn't help but escape again.

t_ch: 就这样我的罪名就像滚雪球一样越滚越大，滚着滚着就被送进了幽囚狱。这次我要是再跑出去，估计就真没地方可以去了。
t_en: That's how my criminal record just started to snowball, and it kept snowballing until I found myself in The Shackling Prison. If I actually do manage to make it out of here, I don't think they'll have anywhere else that can hold me.

t_ch: 你好像很享受越狱？
t_en: You seem to enjoy breaking out of prison?

t_ch: 人嘛总得有点兴趣爱好，有人贪财有人好吃，我这人喜欢的东西有点奇特——越狱上瘾。
t_en: Well, everyone needs hobbies and interests. Some go in search of money, others like fine food, but I'm a little unique... I am addicted to breaking out of prisons.

t_ch: 那种破解难关时的妙趣横生，躲避抓捕时的紧张刺激，重获自由时的酣畅淋漓。啊！世界上还有比越狱更美妙的事么。
t_en: The fun of solving a challenging problem, the excitement of escaping the guards, the joy of regaining your freedom... Ah! Is there anything more wonderful in this world than escaping from prison?

t_ch: 正所谓「越狱一时爽，一直越狱一直爽」，越着越着还搞出了一些名声。有人称我是越狱大师，还抢着要我当我徒弟。
t_en: I have a saying: "Break out of jail once and you get a moment of joy, spend your life jailbreaking and you'll be happy forever!" I've actually become quite renowned for all my escapes. Some call me the "jailbreak master," and some are even begging to be my disciples.

t_ch: 不过又所谓「真正的大师永远怀着一颗学徒的心」，我这不是也来幽囚狱学习深造了么。
t_en: Although, it is also said that: "A true master always maintains the heart of an apprentice." That is precisely why I have come to The Shackling Prison — to further my craft.

t_ch: 你为什么是一只机巧鸟…
t_en: Why are you a cycrane...?

t_ch: 这你就不懂了，越狱是个危险活，我总得给自己找个替身嘛。而且在空中飞得高看得远，更容易发现一些安全破绽。
t_en: You wouldn't understand. Breaking out of prison is a dangerous game. It's best to find myself a substitute body. Plus, being able to fly allows me to see much further, which makes it easier to see gaps in security.

t_ch: 至于这玩意从哪来的…可能是狱卒们废弃的吧，反正我找到的时候它已经破成一坨废铁了，为了修好它废了不少力气。
t_en: As for where I got this thing from... I imagine one of the prison guards chucked it out. Either way, when I found it, it was just a pile of scrap metal. I had to put a lot of work into fixing it.

t_ch: 那你的本体在哪里？
t_en: Then where is your real body?

t_ch: 在机巧鸟身上附身久了，搞得我经常以为这只鸟才是我的本体。至于真正的本体在哪，我自己都忘了。
t_en: I've been possessing this cycrane for so long that I often start to believe that this is my actual body. I don't even remember where my real body is anymore.

t_ch: 不过这不重要，我感觉现在的状态蛮好的，让我回归本体我还不乐意呢。
t_en: But that's not important. I am happy with how I am at the moment. Going back to my real body now wouldn't bring me any joy.

t_ch: 其他犯人看到你的样子不奇怪么？
t_en: Do the other prisoners not think it's weird that you look like this?

t_ch: 幽囚狱里的奇葩还不够多么，什么可以变成其他囚犯样子的变形怪，能思考会说话的玉兆之类的，我这都算是小场面了。
t_en: There's enough weird stuff going on in The Shackling Prison. A shapeshifter that can transform into other prisoners, a Jade Abacus that can think and talk... What I do is not a big deal at all in here.

t_ch: 再说了，我还有一个身份，就是阿合马铁窗集团的首席物流官。犯人们从阿合马那买的东西，还得眼巴巴的等着我给他们送过去呢。
t_en: Plus, I have another role — I'm the chief of logistics for the Arma Correctional Group. Prisoners have to rely on me to deliver whatever they bought from Arma.

t_ch: 哦，你是刚来的，可能不知道阿合马铁窗集团。没关系，后面你就慢慢知道了，在幽囚狱总有需要集团的时候。
t_en: Oh. You're new here, so you probably don't know about the Arma Correctional Group. It's fine. You'll come to understand that, in The Shackling Prison, there will always be times when you need the Group.

t_ch: 有什么越狱秘诀可以传授么？
t_en: Do you have any jailbreaking tips you can share with me?

t_ch: 其实也没么什么秘诀，最关键的就是坚持。有些犯人尝试个一两次就放弃了，而我能为破解一道锁研究好几年。
t_en: Honestly, I don't have any tricks or anything like that. The key is perseverance. Some convicts give up after just one or two attempts, but I can spend years researching how to break just a single lock.

t_ch: 其次就是注意观察，哪怕是再固若金汤的监狱也一定存在破绽，只是有的监狱的破绽很隐秘。
t_en: Another thing is careful observation. Even the most tightly guarded prisons will have weak spots, but some of them are better hidden than others.

t_ch: 就拿幽囚狱来说吧，虽然这里的安全措施已经复杂到令人发指的程度，但那个步离人不是照样跑出去了么……
t_en: Take The Shackling Prison for example. Even though their security measures are so complicated they can drive a man insane, that borisin still managed to escape, no?

t_ch: 幽囚狱的破绽在哪里。
t_en: What are The Shackling Prison's weak spots?

t_ch: 你这个问题问的，我要知道的话不早跑出去了么。
t_en: Well, if I knew that, then I'd have escaped a long time ago, wouldn't I?

t_ch: 不过凭我多年的经验，我觉得幽囚狱最大的安全保障在于环绕四周的水障。要是有精通御水之术之人帮助我，可能会更容易逃出去。
t_en: But, my many years of experience tell me that The Shackling Prison's greatest insurance is the surrounding moat. If only I had someone proficient in water magic with me, my escape would be a lot easier.

t_ch: 步离人越狱的时候你在场？
t_en: Were you here when the borisin escaped?

t_ch: 是啊。那天我正好在送货，远远的看到一群步离人冲杀了出去。
t_en: I was. I happened to be on a delivery that day, but I saw a group of borisin fighting their way out in the distance.

t_ch: 当时场面一片混乱，很多犯人乘机逃了出去。哼，浑水摸鱼算什么好汉，有本事就靠自己的技术出去。
t_en: The entire place was in chaos. Lots of prisoners used it as their chance to escape. Hmph, what kind of hero tries to capitalize on an advantage like that? If you've got the skills, then you should rely on nothing but yourself!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 那我继续越狱了。
t_en: Then I'll get back to my jailbreaking.

t_ch: 你先忙，我要走了。
t_en: I've got to go. I won't disturb you.

t_ch: 那我继续越狱了。
t_en: Then I'll get back to my jailbreaking.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}你又来了啊，正好正好，我刚研究了一种越狱方法，你要不要看看？
t_en: You're back again, {F#girl}{M#boy}. Perfect timing. I've researched a new way to escape this place. You want to see it?

t_ch: …除非是太卜大人亲临，否则其他人等一律止步。我再次声明，卜算推演之外的事，不清楚，不了解，不感兴趣。
t_en: ...Except for the master diviner, no one else has access here. Once again, I'd like to emphasize that I have no knowledge or interest in things unrelated to divination.

t_ch: 重申一遍，现在是元会算法运行期间，闲杂人等请勿打扰。
t_en: Please note that the meta algorithm is running at the moment. Unauthorized personnel should not disturb the process.

t_ch: 别这么不近人情嘛…
t_en: Don't be so rigid...

t_ch: 人情也要看场合，该有的分寸可不能丢。
t_en: Well, it all depends on the situation. I merely act accordingly.

t_ch: 聊聊也不行？
t_en: Can't you have a little chat with me?

t_ch: 那你要聊什么呢？若是和卜算一道有关，我自然是欢迎的，闲聊的话还是免了。
t_en: What do you want to talk about? If it's about divination, I'd be happy to have the conversation. Count me out if you just want to chitchat.

t_ch: 我只是路过的，你别凶我呀。
t_en: I'm just passing by. You don't have to be so mean to me.

t_ch: 我并不是在凶你，只是我正准备推演卦象，容不得外人干扰，请见谅。
t_en: I'm not being mean. I just can't be disturbed when I'm preparing for a hexagrammatic inference. Please don't be bothered by it.

t_ch: 我还有不少事务要处理…你若是有事，不妨直说。
t_en: I have a lot of things on my plate... Could you be more concise with your question, if you don't mind?

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 你想问什么？
t_en: What do you want to know?

t_ch: 你对谁都是这个态度吗？
t_en: Are you like this to everyone?

t_ch: 你要是觉得我是故意的，那我恐怕让你失望了。就算是太卜大人亲临，我也是这样，不会有什么改变。
t_en: I'm afraid you'll be disappointed if you think I'm doing this on purpose. I'd act the same way even if you were the master diviner.

t_ch: 我既身为卜者，应当不骄不躁，不卑不亢…这些话，我时刻记在心中。
t_en: As a diviner, I should remain humble and well-composed. I always keep these words in my mind.

t_ch: 你平时都在忙些什么？
t_en: What do you usually do?

t_ch: 我现在负责打理穷观阵的一处阵法，至于具体的内容…无非是推衍卜算，记录涨落幅度之类的事务。
t_en: I'm now in charge of a matrix at the Matrix of Prescience... My jobs include making divination inferences and recording the changes.

t_ch: 至于阵法的细节，涉及太卜司机密的事…恕我无可奉告。
t_en: I'm afraid I can't disclose the details of the matrix as it involves confidential information about the Divination Commission.

t_ch: 有点好奇，同僚是如何评价你的？
t_en: I'm curious about what your colleagues have to say about you.

t_ch: 这种无关紧要的事，没必要知道，也没必要打听。
t_en: There's no point in asking about meaningless things like that.

t_ch: 他们爱怎么说怎么说，只要阵法一切正常，其他的事都不值一提。
t_en: They can say whatever they want. As long as the matrix works smoothly, other things don't matter.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 慢走不送。
t_en: See you.

t_ch: 那还是不打扰你了。
t_en: Well, I'd better leave you alone then.

t_ch: 慢走不送。
t_en: See you.

t_en: I'm here for business. There are some things I wanted to ask you...

t_ch: 闲着也是闲着，就陪你聊聊吧。
t_en: Well, I've got nothing else to do. We may as well chat for a while.

t_ch: 你是犯了什么事被关进来？
t_en: What did you do to get locked up in here?

t_ch: 具体什么事…呃，我自己都记不清了，但我一不会偷二不会抢，所以肯定犯的不是大事。
t_en: Ah... I don't really, uhhh, remember. But I'm not a robber or a thief, so it can't have been anything serious.

t_ch: 但我这人吧，进了局子就不老实。刚开始他们把我关进地衡司的治安所，那里实在是太无聊了，我没忍住就跑了出来。
t_en: But I just can't sit still. It was when they locked me up that I started misbehaving. At first, they had me locked up in the Realm-Keeping Commission penitentiary, but I was bored to tears, so I had to escape.

t_ch: 这不就喜提越狱罪了么，他们便给我找了看守更严密的监狱。但那地方也没有趣多少，我一没忍住又跑了出来。
t_en: Obviously, that got me arrested for jailbreaking, so they got a prison with tighter security for me. But that place was just as boring. I couldn't help but escape again.

t_ch: 就这样我的罪名就像滚雪球一样越滚越大，滚着滚着就被送进了幽囚狱。这次我要是再跑出去，估计就真没地方可以去了。
t_en: That's how my criminal record just started to snowball, and it kept snowballing until I found myself in The Shackling Prison. If I actually do manage to make it out of here, I don't think they'll have anywhere else that can hold me.

t_ch: 你好像很享受越狱？
t_en: You seem to enjoy breaking out of prison?

t_ch: 人嘛总得有点兴趣爱好，有人贪财有人好吃，我这人喜欢的东西有点奇特——越狱上瘾。
t_en: Well, everyone needs hobbies and interests. Some go in search of money, others like fine food, but I'm a little unique... I am addicted to breaking out of prisons.

t_ch: 那种破解难关时的妙趣横生，躲避抓捕时的紧张刺激，重获自由时的酣畅淋漓。啊！世界上还有比越狱更美妙的事么。
t_en: The fun of solving a challenging problem, the excitement of escaping the guards, the joy of regaining your freedom... Ah! Is there anything more wonderful in this world than escaping from prison?

t_ch: 正所谓「越狱一时爽，一直越狱一直爽」，越着越着还搞出了一些名声。有人称我是越狱大师，还抢着要我当我徒弟。
t_en: I have a saying: "Break out of jail once and you get a moment of joy, spend your life jailbreaking and you'll be happy forever!" I've actually become quite renowned for all my escapes. Some call me the "jailbreak master," and some are even begging to be my disciples.

t_ch: 不过又所谓「真正的大师永远怀着一颗学徒的心」，我这不是也来幽囚狱学习深造了么。
t_en: Although, it is also said that: "A true master always maintains the heart of an apprentice." That is precisely why I have come to The Shackling Prison — to further my craft.

t_ch: 你为什么是一只机巧鸟…
t_en: Why are you a cycrane...?

t_ch: 这你就不懂了，越狱是个危险活，我总得给自己找个替身嘛。而且在空中飞得高看得远，更容易发现一些安全破绽。
t_en: You wouldn't understand. Breaking out of prison is a dangerous game. It's best to find myself a substitute body. Plus, being able to fly allows me to see much further, which makes it easier to see gaps in security.

t_ch: 至于这玩意从哪来的…可能是狱卒们废弃的吧，反正我找到的时候它已经破成一坨废铁了，为了修好它废了不少力气。
t_en: As for where I got this thing from... I imagine one of the prison guards chucked it out. Either way, when I found it, it was just a pile of scrap metal. I had to put a lot of work into fixing it.

t_ch: 那你的本体在哪里？
t_en: Then where is your real body?

t_ch: 在机巧鸟身上附身久了，搞得我经常以为这只鸟才是我的本体。至于真正的本体在哪，我自己都忘了。
t_en: I've been possessing this cycrane for so long that I often start to believe that this is my actual body. I don't even remember where my real body is anymore.

t_ch: 不过这不重要，我感觉现在的状态蛮好的，让我回归本体我还不乐意呢。
t_en: But that's not important. I am happy with how I am at the moment. Going back to my real body now wouldn't bring me any joy.

t_ch: 其他犯人看到你的样子不奇怪么？
t_en: Do the other prisoners not think it's weird that you look like this?

t_ch: 幽囚狱里的奇葩还不够多么，什么可以变成其他囚犯样子的变形怪，能思考会说话的玉兆之类的，我这都算是小场面了。
t_en: There's enough weird stuff going on in The Shackling Prison. A shapeshifter that can transform into other prisoners, a Jade Abacus that can think and talk... What I do is not a big deal at all in here.

t_ch: 再说了，我还有一个身份，就是阿合马铁窗集团的首席物流官。犯人们从阿合马那买的东西，还得眼巴巴的等着我给他们送过去呢。

t_ch: 「一日不可二卦」，要切记，切记！
t_en: "Only one divination per day"! I must bear this in mind!

t_ch: 抱歉，刚才在想别的事情，没有注意到您…哎，今日我不适合占算，就此作罢好了。
t_en: Sorry, my mind was elsewhere just now, and I didn't notice your presence... Well, today is not a good day for divination. Let's do it some other time.

t_ch: 「一日不可二卦」是什么意思？
t_en: What does "only one divination per day" mean?

t_ch: 卜者都是要解决具体的问题，面对占卜要始终保持客观、理性、冷静、平和的方式去处理它，否者占卜就会变成主观臆断。
t_en: Diviners deal with specific issues. We have to maintain an objective, rational, and calm approach to prevent the result from becoming our subjective opinion.

t_ch: 今日为何不适合占算？
t_en: Why isn't today a good day for divination?
